context_text: <A> what i did mention um i was having this conversation well a similar conversation with one of i think it was either my cousin or my mom but um i was saying well it's easier to to call people friends when you met so many people when you were at school because you were forced to interact with them on a daily basis / <B> right / <A> so i think that's also where it come comes from because you know we have a lot of people around us when we're going to school { and we're } / <B> [ uh-huh ] / <A> with them so much we might as well call them our friends even when we know that they're not and { i think } / <B> [ yeah ] / <A> that's also it leads to why there's so many um rumors in high school because we consider everybody our friends but we both know that we talk about people behind their backs just because / <B> aaw / <A> you know they're / <B> oh / <A> there we know a bunch about them / <B> yeah / <A> so but when you're older and you you're starting to have families and when you're you know having a career and stuff like that you can't socially interact with other people and i think that's why we've stopped calling people um friends we { just } / <B> [ uh-huh ] / <A> know oh this is my work acquaintance this is somebody that i you know there's always some it's somebody that i do this with it's not so much oh this is my friend blah blah blah blah / <B> right
